topic: Sudanese food craving — misses mom's mulah

turns.content: man im craving mulah so bad rn. like actual homemade mulah not the restaurant stuff
I can hear the longing in that. There's nothing quite like homemade food — what kind of mulah are you craving specifically?
my moms mulah bamia bro. the okra stew. she makes it different than anyone else wallahi nobody can touch it. the way she does the onions and the spices its just different
Okra stew made the right way is incredible. Every mom has that one dish where the recipe is basically just years of instinct, right? Have you ever tried making it yourself?
bro i tried once and it was so bad lol. like the taste was off and the texture was wrong. she uses dried okra and i used fresh and it wasnt the same at all

Dried okra vs fresh makes a huge difference in texture and flavor — dried gives it that deeper, more concentrated taste. Did you try asking her for the actual steps, or is she one of those cooks who just goes by feel?

bro she literally says a little bit of this a little bit of that. no measurements nothing. i called her once while cooking and she was like just put the spices until it smells right. like ok mama thanks lol

Ha, that's every traditional cook. "You'll know when it's right." Not the most helpful instructions when you're standing in the kitchen lost. Are there any Sudanese restaurants near you that at least get close?
theres a couple spots in the southwest side but honestly they aight. like its food but its not the same. theres this one place on hillcroft thats decent for kisra and mulah but bro its not my moms

Hillcroft has a solid stretch of East African spots. It won't be your mom's cooking, but at least it's something when the craving hits hard. Do you go there often or just when you really can't take it anymore?
only when i really need it. honestly its expensive too like 15-16 bucks for a plate when my mom would make enough for the whole family for that price. but sometimes you just need that taste you know
Yeah, sometimes you just need it regardless of the price. It's not really about the food at that point — it's about what it reminds you of.
wallahi thats exactly it bro. its not just food its like being home again for 20 minutes. maybe i should just try again tho like practice making it until i get it right

Honestly that's not a bad idea. Next time you call your mom, maybe do a video call while she's actually cooking it so you can watch the amounts she uses even if she won't measure them. Sometimes seeing it is better than hearing "a little bit of this."
thats actually smart bro im gonna try that. anyway let me stop being emotional out here lol i got rides to do
Nothing wrong with missing home cooking. Hope you get a good night of rides in. And maybe hit up that Hillcroft spot this week — treat yourself.
